Turkey - Factors To Watch on May 9
data.description:
Here are news, reports and events that may affect Turkish financial markets on Friday.

The lira traded at 38.6795 against the U.S. dollar at 0402 GMT, after closing at 38.61 on Thursday.

The main BIST 100 share index closed 2.25% higher at 9,279.21 points on Thursday.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Japanese stocks jumped on Friday, supported by the dollar's surge against the yen, after a U.S. trade deal with Britain fuelled hopes of progress in tariff talks with other countries.

ERDOGAN PROGRAMME

Turkey's President Tayyip Erdogan will attend a meeting of business group MUSIAD in Istanbul (1130 GMT).

INDUSTRIAL PRODUCTION

Turkey's statistics institute releases industrial production data for March (0700 GMT)

VICE PRESIDENT

Turkey's Vice President Cevdet Yilmaz will speak at the geothermal investment summit in the central Turkish town of Nevsehir (0700 GMT)

INDUSTRY MINISTER

Turkish Industry Minister Mehmet Fatih Kacir will attend an event on climate and economy, sustainable mobility, vehicles and technologies (0700 GMT).

data.title: Oman & Emirates Investment to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Oman & Emirates Investment Holding Co. (ADX:OEIHC) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 12 pm local time on May 12 due to its board meeting.

The investment manager will resume trading after the meeting results are disclosed, according to a Thursday release.

data.title: Australia's ANZ falls; brokerages cut PT on weak outlook
data.description:
** ANZ falls 0.8% to A$29.16; set to snap three-week winning streak with ~4% weekly loss

** Stock underperforms financials' 1.2% rise

** Jefferies trims PT to A$25.95 from A$26.33

** Assumes 10% dividend cut from H2 FY25, with high payout ratio amid expense headwinds

** Morgan Stanley lowers PT by ~6% to A$27.50 on reducing cash EPS estimates for FY26 and FY27 by 1% and 4% respectively due to weaker revenue growth

** UBS cuts PT to A$30 from A$31 after reducing cash earnings estimates by 2.3%, 0.6% and 0.4% for FY25, FY26 and FY27, respectively

** ANZ reports flat H1 cash earnings with pressured margins, elevated gross impaired assets

** Stock gains 3% YTD

data.title: Iraq's SOMO offers fuel oil term supplies for second-half of 2025, sources say
data.description:
Iraq's state-owned marketer SOMO has offered a term contract to supply high-sulphur fuel oil between July and December 2025, according to industry sources.

The fuel oil will be lifted from the Khor al-Zubair (KAZ) ship-to-ship transfer port, with a volume of about 145,000 metric tons per month. The tender will close on May 18, with a validity of up to 20 days.

data.title: NTT 4Q Ebitda Margin 18.8x% Vs 23.1% a Year Earlier
Al Buhaira National Insurance to Halt Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
Al Buhaira National Insurance (ADX:ABNIC) shares will temporarily pause trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange from 11 am local time on May 12 due to its board meeting.

The United Arab Emirates-based insurer will resume trading once its meeting results are published, according to a Thursday notice.

data.title: Malaysia Q1 Construction Output Rises the Least in A Year
data.description: Construction activity in Malaysia increased by 16.6% year-on-year in Q1 of 2025, slowing from a 23.1% surge in Q4 and marking the softest pace since Q1 of 2024.

data.title: DeSoto Resources Says Further Assays Extend Gold Anomaly in Guinea Project; Shares Up 7%
data.description:
DeSoto Resources said assay results from 31 rock chip and 287 dump samples from the Dadjan project in Guinea extended a gold-in-soil anomalism to 3.6 kilometers, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Power auger drilling is underway, with further assay results pending, the filing said.

The company is currently executing early stage target generation across its 14 projects, according to the filing.

Shares rose nearly 7% in afternoon trade Friday.

data.title: CompuGroup Medical Receives Delisting Offer from CVC Capital
data.description:
CompuGroup Medical said Thursday it plans to delist following a public offer from private equity company CVC Capital Partners.

The delisting offer of 22 euros per share for the healthcare software company follows a voluntary public offer completed on May 2, through which CVC gained a 24.27% stake in CompuGroup.

The acceptance period for the delisting offer is expected to commence in May and the deal is expected to close in the first half or before CompuGroup's annual general meeting on Aug. 1.

CompuGroup's supervisory and administrative boards and managing directors plan to recommend that shareholders accept the delisting offer.

data.title: China's Trade Surplus Declines in April; Exports Top Forecasts
data.description:
China's trade surplus declined to $96.2 billion in April, versus $102.6 billion in March, according to data released by China's General Administration of Customs.

The recent figure was higher than the Reuters forecast of $89 billion in trade surplus.

In the first four months of 2025, the trade surplus stood at $368.8 billion.

Exports rose 8.1% to $315.7 billion in April, faster than the 1.9% growth expected in a Reuters poll. Total import value ticked down 0.2% to $219.5 billion, slower than the Reuters forecast of a 5.9% fall.

Total import and export value rose 4.6% year over year to $535.2 billion in April.

data.title: Cantaloupe Inc reports results for the quarter ended March 31 - Earnings Summary
data.description:
• Cantaloupe Inc reported quarterly adjusted earnings of 65 cents​​ per share for the quarter ended March 31, higher than the same quarter last year, when the company reported EPS of 6 cents. The mean expectation of five analysts for the quarter was for earnings of 10 cents per share. Wall Street expected results to range from 9 cents to 10 cents per share.

  • Revenue rose 11.1% to $75.43 million from a year ago; analysts expected $79.83 million.

  • Cantaloupe Inc's reported EPS for the quarter was 65 cents​.

  • The company reported quarterly net income of $48.87 million.

  • Cantaloupe Inc shares had risen by 4.4% this quarter and lost 13.6% so far this year.

FORECAST CHANGES

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 2.1% in the last three months.​

  • In the last 30 days, there have been no negative revisions of earnings estimates

RECOMMENDATIONS

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 6 "strong buy" or "buy," no "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell." The average consensus recommendation for the financial technology (fintech) peer group is also "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Cantaloupe Inc is $12.00

data.title: NTT 4Q Net Profit Missed Y246.7B Expected in Poll of Analysts by LSEG
State Grid Information & Communication's Two Subsidiaries Bag 477 Million Yuan Worth of Bids From China's State Grid
data.description:
State Grid Information & Communication's two subsidiaries won equipment bids from the State Grid Corporation of China for 477 million yuan, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

Anhui Jiyuan Software and Beijing CEC Feihua Communications are yet to sign contracts after winning the State Grids' 23rd batch of procurements this year, the power company said.

data.title: Metals in tight range ahead of China-US trade talks
data.description:
Metal prices in London traded in a tight range on Friday as traders were cautious ahead of the U.S.-China trade talks this weekend.

Benchmark copper on the London Metal Exchange (LME) fell 0.3% to $9,405 a metric ton on the day as of 0346 GMT, but were higher by 0.42% from $9,365.5 last Friday.

U.S. President Donald Trump and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Thursday announced a limited bilateral trade agreement that leaves in place Trump's 10% tariffs on British exports, modestly expands agricultural access for both countries and lowers prohibitive U.S. duties on British car exports.

At the same time, traders adopted a cautious stance ahead of the scheduled U.S.-China meeting in Switzerland this weekend, after months of escalating tensions that pushed tariffs between the world's two largest economies well beyond 100%.

The two countries are expected to discuss easing broader tariffs and duties on specific products.

"The discussions between the U.S. and China this weekend are crucial. Given the unpredictable nature of Trump's stance, we are watching the situation with caution until we see clear indications of advancement," a trader said.

Among other London metals, aluminium lost 0.1% to $2,411 a ton, zinc rose 0.7% to $2,636, lead added 0.8% to $1,960, tin lost 0.6% to $31,695 and nickel was down 0.2% to $15,505 a ton.

The most-traded copper contract on the Shanghai Futures Exchange (SHFE) rose 0.1% to 77,630 yuan ($10,722) per ton, with support from rapidly falling stocks monitored by SHFE due to robust domestic demand.

The Yangshan copper premium (SMM-CUYP-CN), a gauge of China's copper demand, was at $102 a ton on Thursday, its highest since December 2023.

SHFE aluminium added 0.5% to 19,585 yuan a ton, zinc was up 0.2% at 22,325 yuan, lead was up 0.3% at 16,830 yuan, nickel was down 0.2% to 123,420 yuan and tin dropped 0.3% to 259,980 yuan.

($1 = 7.2435 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Naver's Net Profit Suffers as AI Costs, Base Effect Weigh — Update
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun

South Korean internet giant Naver suffered a net profit setback as increased labor and marketing costs for artificial-intelligence services and other new business initiatives weighed on its bottom line despite solid revenue growth.

Strong one-off gains from the disposal of subsidiaries a year earlier also made its profit look weak this year, the company said.

In an earnings call Friday, Chief Executive Choi Soo-yeon remained upbeat about the company's generative AI model, which she said will focus on upgrading its e-commerce and search services to enhance the user experience.

"We're seeing positive results," Choi said, referring to the impact of Naver's own AI tool on businesses.

Choi expects to see Naver's AI tool help generate profit from the second quarter through business-to-business deals. In the first quarter, the company secured a contract from the country's central bank, the Bank of Korea, to provide cloud-computing services with its own large language model, HyperCLOVA X, built in. That followed a similar deal with state utility Korea Hydro & Nuclear Power, Choi said.

Starting as a search engine, Naver has grown to become a digital powerhouse, offering services ranging from online advertising and shopping to mobile payments and paid-content subscriptions. The company in March launched a standalone shopping app, called Naver Plus Store, and an AI-briefing feature.

The developments come as leading tech companies are racing to offer AI-backed services to lock in customers in an increasingly competitive online space. Naver is also betting on the technology to boost growth at its flagship e-commerce and search-platform businesses.

The AI-driven spending increase has eaten into earnings, with the company saying Friday that its first-quarter net profit slumped 24% from a year earlier to 423.70 billion won, equivalent to $301.6 million. Still, that was largely in line with analysts' expectations, according to a FactSet-compiled consensus.

Revenue rose 10% to 2.787 trillion won for the period, and operating profit gained 15% to 505.30 billion won. Online-advertising growth was solid across its AI-backed search and e-commerce platforms. Revenue from its fintech and enterprise businesses also grew strongly.

Naver said the net profit weakness was partly because of higher business costs, including marketing expenses that surged 17%. With labor costs also up, by 8.1%, the company's overall operating expenses increased 9.3% for the quarter.

The bottom line also reflected a higher comparison base last year, when it booked gains from disposing of augmented-reality service provider Naver Z and some other subsidiaries, the company said.

Shares in Naver were recently 1.2% higher on Friday, paring their loss this year to about 4%.

Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com

data.title: Yomiuri: Shionogi to Purchase Pharmaceutical Business From JT
data.description:
By Yuhei Matsumoto

Yomiuri Shimbun Staff Writer

Japanese drugmaker Shionogi & Co. on Wednesday announced it will purchase the pharmaceutical business of Japan Tobacco Inc.

Shionogi plans to conduct a takeover bid for Torii Pharmaceutical Co., which is a subsidiary of JT. The drugmaker will also acquire the pharmaceutical business directly conducted by JT and JT's pharmaceutical subsidiary located in the United States. The total amount of the purchase will be about 160 billion yen.

Meanwhile, JT will withdraw from the pharmaceutical business and concentrate on its main tobacco business.

Shionogi is known for its medications for infectious diseases such as COVID-19. By acquiring Torii, with its forte being in medications for allergies and skin diseases, Shionogi is aiming to bolster its drug development capabilities and expand its sales network.

JT entered the pharmaceutical business in 1987. However, the tobacco company concluded it is difficult to expect growth in the medium to long term amid intensifying global competition in developing new drugs.

"Our rivals in the United States and Europe tend to say, 'Japan's drug development has lost energy recently,'" said Isao Teshirogi, chairman and president of Shionogi, at a press conference in Tokyo. "I want to rebut that by launching Japan-made drugs."

"This was the best choice for further fostering the drug development capabilities we have cultivated," said Koji Shimayoshi, an executive vice president of JT.

The tender offer will run from May 8 to June 18. The purchase price per share will be set at 6,350 yen, about 20% higher than the closing price on May 2 (5,230 yen). Following the tender offer, JT will sell its shares in Torii to Shionogi, as Shionogi plans to make Torii its wholly owned subsidiary. The process is set to be completed in September.

This article is from The Yomiuri Shimbun. Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal were not involved in the creation of this content.

data.title: NTT: To Buy Back Up to Y200.0B of Own Shares From May 12 2025 to March 31 2026
Market Chatter: Smiths Group Begins Pre-marketing for GBP1 Billion Electrical Connectors Division
data.description:
Smiths Group launched the pre-marketing for its electrical connectors division, which could be worth 1 billion pounds sterling, London's Financial Times reported Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan bankers are working on the sale of the Smiths Interconnect business. The sale process is expected to formally start in the coming days, the unnamed sources said.

Some people familiar with the plans said the British engineering company's unit could pique the interest of private equity and trade investors, including Molex, Amphenol, Eaton, Aptiv and ITT.

Smiths Group did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Morningstar flags US tariff headwinds for Australia's IPH; stock falls
data.description:
** IPH falls 1.3%

** Morningstar lowers IPH's fair value estimate to A$5.30/shr from A$5.60/shr

** Says intellectual property services co would face headwinds from prevailing U.S. trade tensions

** Notes trade tensions could prompt clients to prioritise filing patents in primary markets tied to manufacturing or market access, while scaling back on filings in secondary regions served by co

** Cuts fair value estimate, reflecting anticipated softness in new patent filings over medium-term

** Stock rated "buy" on avg; median PT is A$6.49, per data compiled by LSEG

** IPH down 9.2% YTD
